most_likely_causes: Significant data drift in the transaction_amount feature
Concept drift caused by new fraud patterns after the promotional campaign
Introduction of a new payment channel altering feature distributions
evidence: Precision dropped from 0.82 to 0.61 and F1-score from 0.79 to 0.66 after 3 months
A new payment channel was introduced
Fraud patterns changed after a promotional campaign
Feature distribution for transaction_amount shifted significantly
Model was not retrained after launch
Data pipeline logs show no failed jobs
less_likely_causes: Threshold or calibration drift due to unchanged decision threshold
Class‑imbalance shift caused by higher transaction volume
recommended_diagnostics: Run statistical drift tests (e.g., KS test, PSI) on key features, especially transaction_amount and new channel attributes
Compare per‑channel performance (old vs. new payment channel) using recent labeled data
Apply concept‑drift detectors (e.g., ADWIN, DDM) on model predictions over time
Re‑evaluate model calibration on the latest data and plot precision‑recall curves
short_term_actions: Create a fresh validation set from the last 2‑4 weeks and measure current precision/recall per channel
Temporarily adjust the decision threshold to recover precision while monitoring recall
Retrain the model on data that includes the new payment channel and post‑campaign fraud patterns, then redeploy a shadow version for A/B testing
If immediate retraining is not possible, add a simple rule‑based filter for the new channel to reduce false positives
long_term_actions: Implement automated data‑drift monitoring dashboards with alerts for feature distribution shifts
Schedule periodic (e.g., monthly) model retraining pipelines that ingest the latest labeled transactions
Incorporate the new payment channel as a first‑class feature and continuously track its impact on model metrics
Set up a calibration monitoring process to automatically recompute optimal thresholds as data evolves
Maintain a data validation suite that checks for missing values, schema changes, and out‑of‑range values before training
reasoning_summary: The sharp precision loss and shifted transaction_amount distribution point to data drift, while the promotional campaign and new payment channel indicate concept drift. No pipeline failures rule out execution errors. Immediate diagnostics should quantify drift and per‑channel performance, followed by rapid retraining with recent data. Long‑term, automate drift detection, regular retraining, and threshold calibration to keep the fraud model aligned with evolving business conditions.
